title: Nature Counting & Early Math
subject: Early Mathematics & Botany
description: Learn numbers 1 to 10 by counting pinecones, leaves, rocks, and wild berries in nature.
learningObjectives: Count objects from 1 to 10 using natural outdoor items.
Recognize basic geometric shapes in leaves and tree trunks.
Categorize items by color, size, and texture.

lessons.title: Counting Outdoor Treasures
lessons.content: Look around the homestead or forest floor. Can you find 3 pinecones? Count them one by one: 1, 2, 3! Touch each pinecone as you count it to practice 1-to-1 correspondence.

lessons.title: Nature's Shapes
lessons.content: Tree trunks are circles! Pine needles look like straight lines! Rocks can be oval or round. Walk around outdoors and spot 3 round objects and 3 long straight sticks.

flashcards.definition: The woody seed container of a pine tree.
A round shape with no sharp corners, like a cut log.
To name numbers in order to find how many items there are.

questions.question: If you find 2 red autumn leaves and pick up 1 green leaf, how many leaves do you have in total?
What shape is the cross-section of a round tree trunk?
Which of these natural items is best for practice counting 1 to 5?

practicalChallenge.title: Homestead Nature Scavenger Count
practicalChallenge.instruction: Go outside with a basket. Collect 5 small stones, 4 dry leaves, and 3 twigs. Lay them out in lines and count them aloud to a parent or instructor!
